redesignGoal: Turn the current one-screen dashboard into a multi-view lead operations console. The home view should summarize the business state and expose fast entry points. Dedicated pages should handle leads, review/intake, follow-ups, activity/audit, and analytics when the variant direction supports them. Adding and editing should happen through dialogs, drawers, or focused forms instead of sprawling inline panels.
appStructure: Home / dashboard: summary cards, queue health, urgent follow-ups, recently changed leads, and clear shortcuts into specialized workflows.
Leads: searchable/filterable lead workspace with list/table density, selected lead context, and add/edit lead dialogs.
Review / intake: focused pasted transcript or draft-lead review workflow with confidence/missing-field cues.
Follow-ups: due/overdue queue, completion flow, and scheduling/editing dialogs.
Activity / audit: timeline-style history for ingestion, review, status changes, and follow-up completion.
Analytics: lightweight operational metrics if useful for the concept; avoid turning this into a decorative chart page.
workflowExpectations: Preserve existing behavior: ingestion, draft lead review, lead status updates, follow-up creation/completion, metrics, and activity/audit visibility.
Create or adapt navigation so users can move between main summary and specialized pages without losing operational context.
Use dialogs/drawers for add lead, edit lead, create follow-up, edit follow-up, and review actions where practical.
Keep page density high and professional. Avoid marketing layout, hero copy, fake feature explanations, or decoration-first redesigns.
Make mobile usable even if the primary target is desktop operations.

queries: B2B CRM lead dashboard dense operational table filters detail side panel
sales pipeline lead operations dashboard activity history follow ups
AI assisted CRM ingestion review workflow dashboard

variants.name: variant-1-command-center
variants.direction: Lead Ops Command Center: home becomes an operations summary with queue health, aging, SLA pressure, and next actions. Specialized pages should feel like command-center modules: leads table, intake/review desk, follow-up queue, activity log, and lightweight analytics.

variants.name: variant-2-review-desk
variants.direction: Human Review Desk: home emphasizes review workload, draft quality, missing fields, and recently reviewed leads. Create a dedicated review/intake page as the primary workflow and keep lead/follow-up editing in calm, focused dialogs.

variants.name: variant-3-pipeline-radar
variants.direction: Pipeline Radar: home summarizes pipeline movement, status distribution, stuck leads, overdue follow-ups, and activity signals. Dedicated pages should make status lifecycle, follow-up urgency, and lead timelines easy to scan.
